entities.name: RICE
entities.description: The crop grown in wetland soils whose nitrogen nutrition is influenced by mineralizable nitrogen.

entities.name: MINERALIZABLE NITROGEN
entities.description: The pool of nitrogen in soil that can be converted into inorganic forms available for plant uptake.

entities.name: WATERLOGGED CONDITIONS
entities.description: Soil conditions where the soil is saturated with water, typical of wetland rice paddies.

entities.name: AMMONIFICATION
entities.description: The biological process of converting organic nitrogen into ammonium.

entities.name: PHILIPPINES
entities.description: The country from which the 39 diverse soil samples were collected.

entities.name: PH
entities.description: A measure of the acidity or alkalinity of the soil.

entities.name: ORGANIC MATTER
entities.description: The organic component of the soil, including organic carbon and total nitrogen.

entities.name: SOIL TEXTURE
entities.description: The relative proportion of sand, silt, and clay particles in the soil.

entities.name: SIMPLE CORRELATION ANALYSIS
entities.description: A statistical method used to determine the strength and direction of the relationship between two variables.

entities.name: AMMONIUM PRODUCTION
entities.description: The amount of ammonium produced during the process of ammonification.

entities.name: TOTAL NITROGEN
entities.description: The total amount of nitrogen present in the soil.

entities.name: ORGANIC CARBON
entities.description: The carbon contained in the organic matter of the soil.

entities.name: C/N RATIO
entities.description: The ratio of organic carbon to total nitrogen in the soil.

entities.name: CATION EXCHANGE CAPACITY
entities.description: The total capacity of a soil to hold exchangeable cations.

entities.name: CLAY
entities.description: The smallest particle size fraction of soil texture.

entities.name: MULTIPLE REGRESSION ANALYSIS
entities.description: A statistical method used to analyze the relationship between a dependent variable and several independent variables.
